name: config
context: Configure git identity, aliases, editor, diff tools, credentials, and per-repo or global settings
info: Git configuration controls identity (name, email), behavior (default branch, line endings, merge strategy), tooling (editor, diff tool, merge tool), and credentials (SSH keys, credential helpers). Config is layered: system (/etc/gitconfig) < global (~/.gitconfig) < local (.git/config) < worktree (.git/config.worktree). Each level overrides the previous. This context covers the essential config settings every agent and developer needs, including identity setup, useful aliases, credential caching, SSH configuration, and .gitignore patterns. See [[git/commit]] for how identity affects commits, [[git/hooks]] for automating behavior, and [[git/remote]] for credential configuration.
body:
# Git Configuration

Git config controls how git behaves, who you are, and how you interact with tools and remotes.

## Config Levels

```bash
# System (all users) — /etc/gitconfig
git config --system

# Global (current user) — ~/.gitconfig
git config --global

# Local (current repo) — .git/config
git config --local

# Worktree (current worktree) — .git/config.worktree
git config --worktree
```

Priority: worktree > local > global > system. Local settings override global.

## Essential Identity Setup

```bash
# Set your name and email (required for commits)
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "you@example.com"

# Verify
git config user.name
git config user.email

# Per-repo identity (for work vs personal)
cd work-project
git config user.email "you@company.com"
```

## Common Settings

```bash
# Default branch name for new repos
git config --global init.defaultBranch main

# Default editor
git config --global core.editor "code --wait"  # VS Code
git config --global core.editor "vim"
git config --global core.editor "nano"

# Pull strategy
git config --global pull.rebase true

# Auto-correct typos (0.5 second delay)
git config --global help.autocorrect 5

# Colorize output
git config --global color.ui auto

# Line endings
git config --global core.autocrlf input   # macOS/Linux
git config --global core.autocrlf true    # Windows

# Default merge strategy
git config --global merge.ff only  # refuse non-fast-forward merges
```

## Useful Aliases

```bash
# Compact log
git config --global alias.lg "log --oneline --graph --all --decorate"

# Status shorthand
git config --global alias.st "status -sb"

# Amend without editing message
git config --global alias.amend "commit --amend --no-edit"

# Unstage a file
git config --global alias.unstage "restore --staged"

# Last commit
git config --global alias.last "log -1 HEAD --stat"

# Show all aliases
git config --global alias.aliases "config --get-regexp ^alias\\."

# Diff of staged changes
git config --global alias.staged "diff --staged"

# Delete merged branches
git config --global alias.cleanup "!git branch --merged | grep -v '\\*\\|main\\|master' | xargs -n 1 git branch -d"
```

## Credential Management

```bash
# Cache credentials in memory (default 15 minutes)
git config --global credential.helper cache

# Cache for longer
git config --global credential.helper 'cache --timeout=3600'

# Store in plaintext file (less secure)
git config --global credential.helper store

# macOS Keychain
git config --global credential.helper osxkeychain

# Windows Credential Manager
git config --global credential.helper manager

# Linux libsecret
git config --global credential.helper /usr/lib/git-core/git-credential-libsecret
```

## SSH Configuration

```bash
# Generate an SSH key
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "you@example.com"

# Add to ssh-agent
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

# Test connection
ssh -T git@github.com

# Force SSH for all GitHub repos
git config --global url."git@github.com:".insteadOf "https://github.com/"
```

## Commit Signing

```bash
# GPG signing
git config --global user.signingkey YOUR_GPG_KEY_ID
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

# SSH signing (git 2.34+)
git config --global gpg.format ssh
git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
git config --global commit.gpgsign true
```

## .gitignore

Global patterns to ignore across all repos:

```bash
# Set global gitignore
git config --global core.excludesFile ~/.gitignore_global
```

```gitignore
# ~/.gitignore_global
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db
*.swp
*.swo
*~
.env
.env.local
node_modules/
__pycache__/
*.pyc
.idea/
.vscode/settings.json
*.log
```

Per-repo `.gitignore` in the repo root:

```gitignore
# Dependencies
node_modules/
vendor/

# Build output
dist/
build/
*.o
*.so

# Environment
.env
.env.local

# IDE
.idea/
.vscode/
*.swp

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Logs
*.log
logs/
```

## Viewing Config

```bash
# List all settings with origin
git config --list --show-origin

# List global settings
git config --global --list

# Get a specific value
git config user.email

# Edit global config in editor
git config --global --edit
```

## Conditional Includes

Different config for different directories (git 2.13+):

```ini
# ~/.gitconfig
[includeIf "gitdir:~/work/"]
    path = ~/.gitconfig-work

[includeIf "gitdir:~/personal/"]
    path = ~/.gitconfig-personal
```

```ini
# ~/.gitconfig-work
[user]
    email = you@company.com
    signingkey = WORK_KEY_ID
```

This lets you use different identities and signing keys based on where the repo lives.

sections.title: Identity configuration
sections.body: Set `user.name` and `user.email` with `git config --global`. These appear in every commit. Use `--local` (per-repo) to override for work vs personal projects. Use conditional includes (`includeIf`) to auto-switch based on directory. Verify with `git config user.name` and `git config user.email`. Identity is required — commits fail without it.

sections.title: Git aliases for productivity
sections.body: Define shortcuts with `git config --global alias.<name> "<command>"`. Prefix with `!` for shell commands: `alias.cleanup "!git branch --merged | grep -v main | xargs git branch -d"`. Common aliases: `lg` for graph log, `st` for short status, `amend` for `--amend --no-edit`, `unstage` for `restore --staged`. List all with `git config --get-regexp ^alias`.

sections.title: Credential and SSH setup
sections.body: Use credential helpers to avoid re-entering passwords: `cache` (memory, 15min), `store` (plaintext file), `osxkeychain` (macOS), `manager` (Windows). For SSH: generate ed25519 keys, add to agent, add public key to GitHub. Force SSH globally: `git config --global url."git@github.com:".insteadOf "https://github.com/"`. See [[git/remote]] for using credentials during push/pull.

sections.title: Ignoring files with .gitignore
sections.body: Per-repo: create `.gitignore` in repo root. Global: set `core.excludesFile` to a global gitignore path. Patterns use glob syntax: `*` matches anything, `**` matches directories recursively, `!` negates. Common ignores: `node_modules/`, `.env`, `dist/`, `__pycache__/`, `.DS_Store`, IDE configs. Use `git check-ignore -v <file>` to debug which rule ignores a file.

sections.title: Commit signing with GPG or SSH
sections.body: GPG: set `user.signingkey` and `commit.gpgsign true`. SSH (git 2.34+): set `gpg.format ssh`, `user.signingkey` to your `.pub` file, and `commit.gpgsign true`. Signed commits show 'Verified' on GitHub. List GPG keys: `gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format LONG`. See [[git/commit]] for signing individual commits.

links.context: Credentials are used for remote operations
Hooks and config work together for workflow automation

related.note: user.name and user.email determine commit authorship
Hooks and config together control git behavior

author: dojo-community
